title: allow Write(*) + deny Write(**) -> the deny is a silent no-op and the write goes through

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: CRITICAL FOOTGUN: deny Write(**) fails to match (same glob no-op as P3-a on the deny side), so the matching allow Write(*) wins and the write succeeds — uniformly for a cwd file and a subdir file. You believe writes are blocked; they are not, and no error or warning is emitted. The reliably-blocking deny form is Write(*) (P2-b); to protect a directory use Edit(<dir>/**), which hard-covers the Write tool (S9). Write(<dir>/**) and bare Write(**) protect nothing.
